platforms.name: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
details: **AWS Agent Registry** (Apr 9, 2026) — New fully managed discovery service providing a centralized catalog for agents, MCP servers, tools, skills, and custom resources. Supports IAM or JWT authorization, approval workflows for governance, hybrid semantic and keyword search, and an MCP-native endpoint so MCP-compatible clients can query the registry directly. Exposes new entity types (Registries, Registry Records) and integrates with EventBridge and CloudTrail. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/registry.html

platforms.name: Cursor
details: **Cursor CLI Debug Mode, /btw, /config, /statusline** (Apr 14, 2026) — Quality-of-life release for the Cursor CLI. `/debug` drives a hypothesis-generating debugging loop (agent plants log statements, uses runtime information to isolate root cause, then makes a targeted fix). `/btw` lets users ask side questions without derailing the agent's primary task. `/config` opens an interactive settings panel for model and runtime preferences. `/statusline` customizes session signals shown in the status bar. Source: https://cursor.com/docs/agent/debug-mode

findings.title: AWS Agent Registry
findings.description: Entirely new AWS service (April 9, 2026) that acts as the organization-wide catalog for agents, MCP servers, tools, agent skills, and custom resources — deployed inside Amazon Bedrock AgentCore. Introduces multiple new inventory entities that Zenity must discover and map: **Registries** (the catalog itself, optionally scoped by resource type, stage, or business unit), **Registry Records** (individual agent / MCP / tool / skill entries with protocol-validated metadata), **publisher / curator / approval-workflow roles**, and registry-level authorization configuration (IAM or JWT from the customer's IdP). Because the registry exposes an **MCP-native endpoint**, any MCP-compatible agent (Claude, Cursor, Kiro, Amazon Q CLI, Bedrock AgentCore Runtime) can discover and invoke the listed tools — creating a new lateral-movement and shadow-tool risk surface where an overbroad approval setting or an unreviewed record effectively grants enterprise-wide agent access. AISPM should ingest registry contents via the Registry service APIs, correlate records against their downstream runtime deployments, flag records that bypass approval, and track `CloudTrail` events for record publication, approval, and deprecation

findings.title: AWS Agent Registry approval bypass and MCP discovery surface
findings.description: Because AWS Agent Registry is queryable over an MCP endpoint and supports "custom resources" with flexible metadata, AIRT red-teaming scenarios must cover (a) agents discovering and calling MCP tools they were never explicitly granted via a single MCP query against the registry, (b) poisoned record metadata (description, schema) used as a prompt-injection vector against AI consumers of the registry, and (c) unapproved/draft records that leak through misconfigured approval settings

findings.title: Cursor CLI Debug Mode (autonomous hypothesis-driven debugging)
findings.description: Cursor CLI 0.122.x (April 14, 2026) introduces `/debug`, a self-directed debugging loop where the agent generates hypotheses, edits the codebase to add log statements, runs the program, and iterates until it pinpoints a root cause before issuing a fix. This is a new local-agent capability that writes to files and executes code repeatedly without per-step user approval. `/btw` also allows out-of-band side queries that may read unrelated project files mid-task. ENDPOINT monitoring must inventory CLI sessions that invoke `/debug` (potentially large diff surface from log insertions), track `/statusline` custom scripts as a potential code-exec surface, and flag `/config` changes that switch underlying model providers

platforms.name: Claude Enterprise (Anthropic)
details: **Claude Opus 4.7** (Apr 16, 2026) — New model with "stronger performance across coding, agents, vision, and multi-step tasks." Already rolling into partner platforms (Vertex AI, others). Source: https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-7

**Claude Design by Anthropic Labs** (Apr 17, 2026) — New product for collaborating with Claude to create "designs, prototypes, slides, one-pagers, and more." Expands Claude surface beyond chat/code into design artifact generation. Source: https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-design-anthropic-labs

platforms.name: ChatGPT Enterprise (OpenAI)
details: **Codex expands beyond coding** (Apr 16, 2026) — Major workspace update adds in-app browser (local/public page previews), computer use for macOS app operation, chats without project folders, thread automations (scheduled wake-ups), pull request workflow integration, artifact viewer for generated files (PDFs/spreadsheets/docs/slides), task sidebar, and 90+ new plugins. Source: https://developers.openai.com/codex/app/computer-use

**Codex CLI 0.121.0** (Apr 15, 2026) — Marketplace installation from GitHub/git URLs, TUI history reverse search, memory mode controls, MCP Apps tool calls, parallel-call opt-in, realtime/app-server APIs for output modality and transcript events, secure devcontainer profile with bubblewrap. Source: https://developers.openai.com/codex/plugins

**OpenAI Python SDK v2.32.0** (Apr 15, 2026) — Event handler implementation for WebSockets, reconnection support in WebSockets, OAuthErrorCode type addition, InputFileContent detail field. Source: https://github.com/openai/openai-python/releases

platforms.name: Google Vertex AI
details: **RAG Cross Corpus Retrieval (public preview)** (Apr 17, 2026) — Enables simultaneous retrieval from multiple knowledge bases (RAG corpora). Source: https://docs.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/rag-engine/cross-corpus-retrieval
**Claude Opus 4.7 in Model Garden** (Apr 14, 2026) — New partner model. Source: https://docs.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/release-notes

**Gemma 4 26B A4B IT (experimental)** (Apr 3, 2026) — New multimodal Gemma 4 model in experimental availability. Source: https://docs.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/maas/google/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it

**RAG Engine Serverless mode (public preview)** (Apr 3, 2026) — Fully managed database for RAG resources that abstracts away database provisioning. Source: https://docs.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/rag-engine/serverless-mode
**Schema-based metadata filtering for RAG Engine** (Apr 6, 2026) — New filtering capability for context retrieval. Source: https://docs.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/release-notes
**Colab Enterprise Visualization cells (GA)** (Apr 13, 2026) and **SQL cells (GA)** (Apr 10, 2026). Source: https://docs.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/release-notes

platforms.name: Cursor
details: **Canvases** (Apr 15, 2026) — New interactive canvas artifact type with dashboards and custom interfaces using tables, boxes, diagrams, and charts. Source: https://cursor.com/docs/agent/tools/canvas

**Cursor 3.1** (Apr 13, 2026) — Tiled Layout (pane-based parallel agent management), Upgraded Voice Input (batch speech-to-text), Branch Selection for cloud agents, Diff to File Navigation, Include/exclude Search Filters. Source: https://cursor.com/changelog/3-1

platforms.name: Windsurf
details: **Windsurf 2.0 Launch with Devin cloud agent integration** (Apr 15, 2026) — Devin cloud agent now available directly inside Windsurf, included with every self-serve plan. New Agent Command Center with Kanban-style task organization. Spaces organizational unit. Source: https://windsurf.com/blog/windsurf-2-0

platforms.name: Perplexity
details: **`/v1/models` endpoint** (April 2026) — New GET /v1/models endpoint lists all available Agent API models in OpenAI-compatible format. Source: https://docs.perplexity.ai/changelog

**`/v1/agent` canonical endpoint + new third-party models** (March 2026) — Agent API canonical endpoint is now `/v1/agent`; added support for GPT-5.4, NVIDIA Nemotron, Claude Sonnet 4.6, and Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview as third-party models. Source: https://docs.perplexity.ai/docs/agent-api/quickstart
**AWS Marketplace** (April 2026) — Perplexity API credits now purchasable through AWS Marketplace. Source: https://docs.perplexity.ai/changelog
**n8n Integration** (April 2026) — Native n8n node with Chat Completions, Agent, Search, Embeddings support. Source: https://docs.perplexity.ai/changelog

findings.title: Anthropic Claude Opus 4.7
findings.description: New frontier Anthropic model released April 16, 2026 with stronger coding, agent, vision, and multi-step task capabilities. Already rolling out to Vertex AI Model Garden (April 14) and will appear on Amazon Bedrock and Microsoft Foundry. Ships with automated cybersecurity safeguards that detect and block high-risk cyber requests, and Anthropic specifically trained it to "differentially reduce" offensive-cyber capabilities. AISPM must add Claude Opus 4.7 to model inventory across all integrated platforms and track its deployment scope, since agent-use characteristics differ from prior Opus versions

findings.title: Anthropic Claude Design (Anthropic Labs)
findings.description: New Claude product (April 17, 2026) powered by Claude Opus 4.7 that generates designs, prototypes, slides, and one-pagers — expanding Claude's surface from chat/code into design-artifact creation. During onboarding, Claude reads your codebase and design files to build a team design system automatically; every project then uses those colors, typography, and components. Introduces new entity types for AISPM to track: Claude Design workspaces, team design systems (ingested from codebase), generated prototype/deck artifacts, and Canva/PPTX export sinks. Zenity needs to map how enterprise codebase and brand content flows into Claude Design. Available in research preview on Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise

findings.title: OpenAI Codex expands beyond coding (in-app browser, computer use macOS, PR workflow, 90+ new plugins)
findings.description: Major April 16, 2026 Codex update dramatically expands attack surface. New capabilities include in-app browser (live web interaction from the agent), computer use for macOS ("seeing, clicking, and typing with its own cursor" across any native app, with multiple agents in parallel), GitHub PR workflow integration (direct diff review and comment), thread automations (scheduled autonomous follow-ups), artifact viewer (PDFs/spreadsheets/docs/slides), and 90+ new plugins including Atlassian Rovo, CircleCI, GitLab Issues, Microsoft tools. Each is a new vector for prompt injection, agent-driven data exfiltration, and unauthorized action. AIRT threat models must cover browser-in-agent, macOS computer use, and scheduled-task invocation

findings.title: OpenAI Codex Marketplace Plugin Installation from arbitrary git URLs
findings.description: Codex CLI 0.121.0 (April 15, 2026) added support for installing plugin marketplaces from GitHub, git URLs, local directories, and direct marketplace.json URLs. This introduces a supply-chain surface: developers can install third-party Codex extensions that run locally with the agent's permissions — any repo can ship skills, tools, and MCP servers that the agent will then invoke. ENDPOINT monitoring should inventory installed marketplace plugins and flag unvetted sources

findings.title: Google Vertex AI RAG Cross Corpus Retrieval (public preview)
findings.description: New April 17, 2026 feature enables agentic retrieval across multiple RAG corpora in a single query. Backed by two new APIs: `AsyncRetrieveContexts` (long-running) and `AskContexts` (synchronous answer generation). Uses an Orchestrator/Planning Agent that reads each corpus description to decide where to search — so corpus descriptions become a new security-relevant metadata field. AISPM must discover cross-corpus configurations, map which corpora an agent is authorized to aggregate, and flag overly permissive corpus descriptions. Currently us-central1 only

findings.title: Google Vertex AI RAG Engine Serverless Mode
findings.description: New April 3, 2026 public-preview deployment mode for RAG Engine — fully managed Vector Search 2.0 collection provisioned automatically, no infrastructure configuration. Configured via `GetRagEngineConfig`/`UpdateRagEngineConfig` APIs at the project level. AISPM must discover Serverless-mode RAG corpora as a distinct inventory entity separate from Spanner-mode corpora, and track `UpdateRagEngineConfig` events as governance-relevant changes

findings.title: Google Vertex AI Gemma 4 26B A4B IT (experimental)
findings.description: New multimodal Gemma 4 MoE model (April 3, 2026) added to Vertex AI Model Garden. 26B total parameters but only 3.8B active per inference step; accepts text and image input. AISPM model inventory must track Gemma 4 deployments; multimodal image input expands the prompt-injection surface beyond text

findings.title: Windsurf 2.0 with Embedded Devin Cloud Agent
findings.description: Windsurf 2.0 (April 15, 2026) ships with Devin cloud agent embedded directly inside the Windsurf IDE, included with every self-serve plan (Pro, Max, Teams). Adds an Agent Command Center (Kanban board for local + cloud agents) and a new "Spaces" organizational unit grouping agent sessions, PRs, files, and context by task. Devin runs on its own VM with desktop, browser, and computer-use capabilities — it can continue working after the user closes their laptop. ENDPOINT monitoring must now treat Windsurf-resident Devin invocations as a distinct agent identity that can spawn cloud VMs and execute multi-step tasks independently

findings.title: Cursor Canvases and Cursor 3.1 Tiled Layout
findings.description: Cursor 3.1 (April 13, 2026) adds Tiled Layout for running/managing several agents in parallel panes; Canvases (April 15) are a new durable artifact type rendered alongside chat — agents can generate interactive React interfaces containing charts, tables, diff views, and custom logic via user-written Skills. Canvas Skills (e.g. Docs Canvas Skill on Cursor Marketplace) define what kind of canvas an agent can generate, creating a new inventory entity: Canvas Skills as a form of agent-granted capability. ENDPOINT must track per-session concurrent agents, inventory canvas artifacts, and flag custom Canvas Skill installations

findings.title: Perplexity `/v1/agent` canonical endpoint + `/v1/models`
findings.description: Perplexity promoted `/v1/agent` to the canonical Agent API endpoint (March 2026) — replacing the previous `/v1/responses` path (still accepted as an OpenAI-compatible alias). New `GET /v1/models` endpoint (April 2026) lists all available Agent API models in OpenAI-compatible format. Added third-party models: GPT-5.4, NVIDIA Nemotron, Claude Sonnet 4.6, Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview — so a single Perplexity call can now invoke an OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, or NVIDIA model with built-in `web_search` and `fetch_url` tools. AIDR activity monitoring must recognize `/v1/agent`, attribute cross-provider model usage correctly, and inventory Perplexity as a new multi-provider inference gateway
